name: Agent Loop - Run Approved Task Pack

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      taskpack:
        description: Paste the approved dual-plane Task Pack generated by ChatGPT
        required: true
        type: string
      dry_run:
        description: Validate and plan only; do not implement or merge
        required: false
        default: false
        type: boolean
  issues:
    types: [opened, edited, labeled]
  repository_dispatch:
    types: [agent_loop_taskpack]

permissions:
  contents: write
  issues: write
  pull-requests: write
  actions: read
  checks: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event_name == 'issues' && github.event.issue.number || github.run_id }}
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  run-approved-taskpack:
    if: >-
      github.event_name != 'issues' ||
      (
        github.event.issue.body &&
        contains(github.event.issue.body, 'AGENT_LOOP_METADATA') &&
        !contains(toJson(github.event.issue.labels), 'agent:running') &&
        !contains(toJson(github.event.issue.labels), 'agent:done') &&
        !contains(toJson(github.event.issue.labels), 'agent:blocked') &&
        (
          github.event.issue.user.login == github.repository_owner ||
          github.event.issue.author_association == 'OWNER' ||
          github.event.issue.author_association == 'MEMBER' ||
          github.event.issue.author_association == 'COLLABORATOR'
        ) &&
        (
          github.event.action == 'opened' ||
          github.event.action == 'edited' ||
          (github.event.action == 'labeled' && github.event.label.name == 'agent:run')
        )
      )
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      TASKPACK_PATH: /tmp/agent_loop/taskpack.md
      RAW_TASKPACK_PATH: /tmp/agent_loop/taskpack.raw.md
      WORK_DIR: agent-loop-work
      AUDIT_ISSUE: ${{ github.event_name == 'issues' && github.event.issue.number || '' }}
      DRY_RUN: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.dry_run || false }}
      GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
      AUTOFIX_LOOPS_USED: "0"
      MAX_AUTOFIX_LOOPS: "0"
      VALIDATION_STATUS: FAIL
      CHANGED_FILES_STATUS: FAIL
      PLAN_STATUS: FAIL
      ROUTING_STATUS: FAIL
      TASKPACK_STATUS: FAIL
      NEEDS_AUTOFIX: "false"
      REVIEW_NEEDS_AUTOFIX: "false"
      EXECUTOR_MODE: ""
      ENABLE_CODEX_REVIEW: "false"
      ENABLE_ARCHITECT_REVIEW: "false"
      MAX_PAID_CODEX_CALLS: "0"
      PAID_CALLS_ATTEMPTED: "0"
      VALUE_SCORE: "0"
      ROI_BUDGET_USD: "0"
      RERUN_ALLOWED: "false"
      NEXT_RECOMMENDED_ACTION: "Review failure artifacts before rerun."

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Normalize approved Task Pack
        env:
          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
          EVENT_ACTION: ${{ github.event.action }}
          WORKFLOW_TASKPACK: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.taskpack || '' }}
          ISSUE_BODY: ${{ github.event_name == 'issues' && github.event.issue.body || '' }}
          ISSUE_LABEL_NAME: ${{ github.event_name == 'issues' && github.event.label.name || '' }}
          ISSUE_LABELS_JSON: ${{ github.event_name == 'issues' && toJson(github.event.issue.labels) || '[]' }}
          DISPATCH_TASKPACK: ${{ github.event_name == 'repository_dispatch' && github.event.client_payload.taskpack || '' }}
          DISPATCH_TITLE: ${{ github.event_name == 'repository_dispatch' && github.event.client_payload.title || '' }}
          DISPATCH_SOURCE: ${{ github.event_name == 'repository_dispatch' && github.event.client_payload.source || '' }}
        run: |
          mkdir -p /tmp/agent_loop "$WORK_DIR"
          python3 - <<'PY'
          import json
          import os
          import re
          from pathlib import Path

          meta_re = re.compile(
              r"<!--\s*AGENT_LOOP_METADATA\s*(.*?)\s*END_AGENT_LOOP_METADATA\s*-->",
              re.DOTALL,
          )
          event_name = os.environ["EVENT_NAME"]
          event_action = os.environ.get("EVENT_ACTION", "")
          taskpack = ""
          mode = event_name

          if event_name == "workflow_dispatch":
              taskpack = os.environ.get("WORKFLOW_TASKPACK", "")
          elif event_name == "issues":
              labels = json.loads(os.environ.get("ISSUE_LABELS_JSON") or "[]")
              label_names = {str(item.get("name", "")) for item in labels if isinstance(item, dict)}
              if event_action not in {"opened", "edited", "labeled"}:
                  raise SystemExit("issues trigger ignored because action is not opened, edited, or labeled")
              blocked = {"agent:running", "agent:done", "agent:blocked"}
              present_blocked = sorted(blocked & label_names)
              if present_blocked:
                  raise SystemExit(f"issues trigger blocked by state labels: {', '.join(present_blocked)}")
              taskpack = os.environ.get("ISSUE_BODY", "")
              match = meta_re.search(taskpack)
              if not match:
                  raise SystemExit("issue body must contain AGENT_LOOP_METADATA")
              try:
                  metadata = json.loads(match.group(1))
              except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
                  raise SystemExit(f"metadata JSON parse failed: {exc}") from exc
              if not isinstance(metadata, dict):
                  raise SystemExit("metadata must be a JSON object")
              if metadata.get("source") != "chatgpt-approved":
                  raise SystemExit("issue Task Pack metadata source must be chatgpt-approved")
          elif event_name == "repository_dispatch":
              if event_action != "agent_loop_taskpack":
                  raise SystemExit("repository_dispatch action must be agent_loop_taskpack")
              payload_source = os.environ.get("DISPATCH_SOURCE", "")
              if payload_source and payload_source != "chatgpt-approved":
                  raise SystemExit("repository_dispatch source must be chatgpt-approved when provided")
              taskpack = os.environ.get("DISPATCH_TASKPACK", "")
          else:
              raise SystemExit(f"unsupported event: {event_name}")

          if "AGENT_LOOP_METADATA" not in taskpack:
              raise SystemExit(f"{mode} Task Pack must contain AGENT_LOOP_METADATA")
          Path("/tmp/agent_loop/taskpack.raw.md").write_text(taskpack, encoding="utf-8")
          Path("agent-loop-work/ingestion-mode.txt").write_text(mode + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
          Path("agent-loop-work/repository-dispatch-title.txt").write_text(
              os.environ.get("DISPATCH_TITLE", "") + "\n",
              encoding="utf-8",
          )
          print(f"INGESTION_MODE={mode}")
          PY

      - name: Acquire issue live lock
        id: preflight
        env:
          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
          ISSUE_NUMBER: ${{ github.event_name == 'issues' && github.event.issue.number || '' }}
        run: |
          mkdir -p "$WORK_DIR"
          if [ "$EVENT_NAME" != "issues" ]; then
            echo "should_continue=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "PRECHECK_STATUS=CONTINUE" > "$WORK_DIR/preflight-summary.md"
            exit 0
          fi

          labels_json="$(gh issue view "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --json labels)"
          export LIVE_ISSUE_LABELS_JSON="$labels_json"
          python3 - <<'PY' > "$WORK_DIR/live-labels.txt"
          import json
          import os

          payload = json.loads(os.environ.get("LIVE_ISSUE_LABELS_JSON") or "{}")
          labels = payload.get("labels") or []
          names = sorted(
              str(item.get("name", ""))
              for item in labels
              if isinstance(item, dict) and str(item.get("name", "")).strip()
          )
          print("\n".join(names))
          PY

          blocked_labels="$(grep -E '^(agent:running|agent:done|agent:blocked)$' "$WORK_DIR/live-labels.txt" || true)"
          if [ -n "$blocked_labels" ]; then
            {
              echo "PRECHECK_STATUS=DUPLICATE_IGNORED"
              echo "Issue #${ISSUE_NUMBER} already has an Agent Loop state label."
              echo
              echo "$blocked_labels"
            } > "$WORK_DIR/preflight-summary.md"
            echo "should_continue=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            cat "$WORK_DIR/preflight-summary.md"
            exit 0
          fi

          gh label create "agent:run" --color "5319e7" --description "Agent Loop run" || true
          gh label create "agent:running" --color "1d76db" --description "Agent Loop is running" || true
          gh label create "agent:done" --color "0e8a16" --description "Agent Loop completed" || true
          gh label create "agent:blocked" --color "b60205" --description "Agent Loop blocked" || true
          gh label create "source:chatgpt-approved" --color "0e8a16" --description "Approved ChatGPT Task Pack" || true
          gh label create "risk:T1" --color "fbca04" --description "Agent Loop risk tier T1" || true
          gh label create "risk:T2" --color "fbca04" --description "Agent Loop risk tier T2" || true
          gh issue edit "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --add-label "source:chatgpt-approved" || true
          gh issue edit "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --add-label "agent:run" || true
          gh issue edit "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --add-label "agent:running" || true

          {
            echo "PRECHECK_STATUS=CONTINUE"
            echo "Issue #${ISSUE_NUMBER} acquired agent:running live lock."
          } > "$WORK_DIR/preflight-summary.md"
          echo "should_continue=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          cat "$WORK_DIR/preflight-summary.md"

      - name: Stop duplicate issue trigger
        if: ${{ steps.preflight.outputs.should_continue == 'false' }}
        run: |
          cat "$WORK_DIR/preflight-summary.md" >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"

      - name: Upload duplicate trigger artifact
        if: ${{ steps.preflight.outputs.should_continue == 'false' }}
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: agent-loop-duplicate-${{ github.run_id }}
          path: agent-loop-work/preflight-summary.md

      - name: Prepare Agent Loop labels
        if: ${{ steps.preflight.outputs.should_continue == 'true' }}
        run: |
          gh label create "agent:run" --color "5319e7" --description "Agent Loop run" || true
          gh label create "agent:running" --color "1d76db" --description "Agent Loop is running" || true
          gh label create "agent:done" --color "0e8a16" --description "Agent Loop completed" || true
          gh label create "agent:blocked" --color "b60205" --description "Agent Loop blocked" || true
          gh label create "source:chatgpt-approved" --color "0e8a16" --description "Approved ChatGPT Task Pack" || true
          gh label create "risk:T1" --color "fbca04" --description "Agent Loop risk tier T1" || true
          gh label create "risk:T2" --color "fbca04" --description "Agent Loop risk tier T2" || true

      - name: Resolve audit issue
        if: ${{ steps.preflight.outputs.should_continue == 'true' }}
        id: audit
        env:
          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
          ISSUE_NUMBER: ${{ github.event_name == 'issues' && github.event.issue.number || '' }}
        run: |
          python3 - <<'PY' > "$WORK_DIR/raw-title.txt"
          from pathlib import Path
          text = Path("/tmp/agent_loop/taskpack.raw.md").read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
          for line in text.splitlines():
              if line.startswith("# "):
                  print(line[2:].strip()[:120] or "Approved Task Pack")
                  break
          else:
              print("Approved Task Pack")
          PY
          title="$(cat "$WORK_DIR/raw-title.txt")"
          if [ "$EVENT_NAME" = "repository_dispatch" ]; then
            dispatch_title="$(cat "$WORK_DIR/repository-dispatch-title.txt")"
            if [ -n "$dispatch_title" ]; then
              title="$dispatch_title"
            fi
          fi

          if [ "$EVENT_NAME" = "issues" ]; then
            echo "AUDIT_ISSUE=${ISSUE_NUMBER}" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
            echo "issue_number=${ISSUE_NUMBER}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            gh issue edit "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --add-label "source:chatgpt-approved" || true
            gh issue edit "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --add-label "agent:run" || true
            gh issue edit "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --add-label "agent:running" || true
            gh issue comment "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --body "Agent Loop run started from this approved Task Pack issue: $GITHUB_SERVER_URL/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/actions/runs/$GITHUB_RUN_ID"
          else
            {
              echo "Workflow run: $GITHUB_SERVER_URL/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/actions/runs/$GITHUB_RUN_ID"
              echo
              echo "Ingestion mode: $EVENT_NAME"
              echo
              echo "Raw approved Task Pack:"
              echo
              cat "$RAW_TASKPACK_PATH"
            } > "$WORK_DIR/audit-issue-body.md"
            issue_url="$(gh issue create \
              --title "[Agent Loop] ${title}" \
              --body-file "$WORK_DIR/audit-issue-body.md" \
              --label "agent:run" \
              --label "agent:running" \
              --label "source:chatgpt-approved")"
            issue_number="${issue_url##*/}"
            echo "AUDIT_ISSUE=${issue_number}" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
            echo "issue_number=${issue_number}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            gh issue edit "$issue_number" --add-label "agent:running" || true
          fi

      - name: Autofill Task Pack routing metadata
        if: ${{ steps.preflight.outputs.should_continue == 'true' }}
        run: |
          set +e
          python3 scripts/agent_loop/autofill_taskpack_metadata.py \
            --input "$RAW_TASKPACK_PATH" \

payload.name: exec

# Architect Review Prompt

---2---
# Codex Review Prompt

You are reviewing an Agent Loop PR.

Use the approved Task Pack, diff, validation output, and acceptance criteria
appended below. Do not generate a new Task Pack. Do not modify files.

Check:

- scope drift
- unrelated refactor
- missing tests
- missing validation
- security/privacy risk
- performance regression
- governance mismatch
- forbidden paths
- release/rollback risk
- acceptance criteria mismatch

## Codex Review

REVIEW_STATUS=PASS or FAIL

Unresolved P0/P1: yes/no

## Findings

List findings by severity P0, P1, P2, P3. If no findings, state that clearly.

## Merge Recommendation

Recommend merge or block.

payload.message:
S02-P2 子审计完成。固定基准仍为 `origin/main@07a6e50d593c7b9c74b8f3870b614be86a87160d`。

核心结论：非 OpenAIDatabase 的 3,266 个 P1 候选，建议从 P1 的 `keep 2447 / migrate 793 / merge 26` 收敛为：

| 建议 disposition | 数量 | 边界 |
|---|---:|---|
| `keep` | 2,765 | 2,447 个 active-project 候选＋318 个共享/root 活跃、release-linked 或 owner-bound 路径 |
| `migrate` | 494 | 历史 root governance manifest/taskpack/review bundle；必须成批迁移引用 |
| `merge` | 7 | 无直接运行引用的 Agent Loop 人类说明/示例 |
| `delete/regenerate` | 0 | 本轮没有任何项目达到安全删除或重生成证明 |
| `approval` | 全部 `pending` | 未授权 S03 变更 |

## 共享 governance：790 项

静态扫描发现 497/790 个候选存在逐路径精确引用；其余仍可能由目录 glob、计算路径、owner 工具或外部运行态动态读取。

| 路径族 | 数量 | 精确被引用 | 建议 |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `governance/run_manifests` | 567 | 285 | 118 个绑定 `FINAL_ACCEPTANCE_BUNDLE`、根 HANDOFF 或 CF-L2 当前证据，`keep`；其余 449 个 `migrate`，但动态引用仍为 UNKNOWN |
| `governance/archive` | 112 | 101 | 全部 `keep`；属于 Alpha/EVA_OS/KM_IDSystem 等 owner source/archive 边界 |
| `governance/stage_gates` | 64 | 64 | 全部 `keep`；工具、测试和 manifest 形成运行/回归链 |
| `governance/taskpacks` | 30 | 30 | `migrate`；与 `GOV-M1-1-CORRECTIVE-ACCEPTANCE-20260626.json` 和治理测试原子迁移 |
| `governance/review_bundles` | 15 | 15 | `migrate`；与上述 M1.1 manifest/taskpack 同批处理 |
| `governance/ci_attestations` | 1 | 1 | `keep`；`validate_governance_sync.py` 消费该 binding |
| `governance/decision_policies` | 1 | 1 | `keep`；`generate_governance_dashboard.py` 将其定义为 canonical policy |

关键证据：

- `governance/run_manifests` 在目录外有 150 条路径族引用、涉及 21 个文件；`lean_governance.py` 和 `validate_governance_sync.py` 还会按目录/changed-path 动态处理。
- `stage_gates` 有 592 条目录外引用、涉及 47 个文件；`wave1_gate.py`、`wave2_gate.py`、`structure_audit.py` 等使用固定输入/输出路径。
- `archive` 有 356 条目录外引用。11 个没有逐路径精确引用的文件全部属于 KM_IDSystem 的历史原件/备份，仍受 owner 边界保护。
- `taskpacks` 30 项和 `review_bundles` 15 项全部被同一个历史验收 manifest 列举，并被治理测试断言；不能单独删除。
- `governance/run_manifests/CF-L[REDACTED_PHONE].json` 被 P1 标为 `migrate`，但它绑定当前 Cloudflare validator、测试和 `FINAL_ACCEPTANCE_BUNDLE/cloudflare_compatibility_envelope`，应改为 `keep`。

## Root/shared：29 项

建议 `keep 22 / merge 7`。

必须改为 `keep`：

- 12 个 root scripts 与 `tools/validate_task_pack.py`。全部有 workflow、import、测试、治理注册或 final-command 引用。
- `.github/workflows/agent-loop-run-approved-taskpack.yml`：当前 GitHub Actions 工作流，直接调用 `autofill_taskpack_metadata.py`、`extract_taskpack.py`、`validate_taskpack.py`、`validate_plan.py` 等。
- `.github/codex/prompts/codex-review.md`：工作流直接读取。
- `.codex/automation_state/daily-maintenance-handoff.md`：无静态引用，但可能由 Codex Desktop 动态消费，必须 `keep/UNKNOWN`。
- 以下 6 个文档有直接治理引用或 required-file 约束：

  - `docs/governance/agent_loop/RUN_APPROVED_TASKPACK.md`
  - `TASKPACK_ROUTING_POLICY.md`
  - `TASK_PACK_DUAL_PLANE_SPEC.md`
  - `TASK_PACK_TEMPLATE.md`
  - `examples/minimal_t1_taskpack.md`
  - `docs/governance/templates/开发记录.template.md`

可进入 `merge`，但不可直接删除：

- `.github/codex/prompts/architect-review.md`
- `NEW_CHATGPT_HANDOFF_PROMPT.md`
- `REVIEW_CHECKLIST.md`
- `STAGE_C2_C3_D1_USAGE.md`
- `examples/invalid_missing_project_taskpack.md`
- `examples/minimal_t1_taskpack_escape_slash.md`
- `examples/minimal_t1_taskpack_zh.md`

建议分别合入 `scripts/agent_loop/README.md`、Agent Loop README/dual-plane spec 和唯一最小示例，并同步修复链接后再验收。

## 其他 active-project：2,447 项

全部维持 `keep/pending`。13 个项目均注册于 `governance/projects.yaml`；P1 已单独保护 77 个 active owner entries。

| 项目 | 候选 | documents / scripts / generated / governance / validation |
|---|---:|---|
| PFI | 821 | 145 / 22 / 639 / 0 / 15 |
| KMFA | 583 | 216 / 283 / 70 / 2 / 12 |
| arxiv-daily-push | 401 | 394 / 0 / 4 / 0 / 3 |
| QBVS | 181 | 128 / 6 / 47 / 0 / 0 |
| KM_IDSystem | 167 | 162 / 1 / 0 / 0 / 4 |
| FIFA | 141 | 5 / 0 / 136 / 0 / 0 |
| Serenity-Alipay | 77 | 26 / 18 / 31 / 2 / 0 |
| EEI | 72 | 24 / 15 / 9 / 0 / 24 |
| Alpha | 2 | 0 / 0 / 2 / 0 / 0 |
| WDA | 1 | 1 / 0 / 0 / 0 / 0 |
| whkmSalary | 1 | 1 / 0 / 0 / 0 / 0 |

其中包含 1,713 个 stage/review 标签命中、938 个 generated/snapshot、193 个 exact-duplicate、184 个 handoff、159 个 TaskPack；这些只是信号，不能越过 owner/source 边界。

## 必须继续保护

P1 已正确排除：

- current `FINAL_ACCEPTANCE_BUNDLE` 30 项；
- 根 HANDOFF 2 项；
- current deployment config 7 项；
- active-project owner entries 77 项；
- `AGENTS.md` 与 `governance/projects.yaml` 两个 root governance required 项。

静态引用进一步确认：

- 114 个 run manifest 引用 `FINAL_ACCEPTANCE_BUNDLE`；
- 26 个引用 `HANDOFF/00_下一Agent先读.md`；
- `FINAL_ACCEPTANCE_BUNDLE` 被 final-command tools、dashboard generator、Cloudflare 当前 manifest 广泛读取；
- `HANDOFF/00` 是 final-bundle prerequisite 和 validator 输入；
- Agent Loop workflow 是事件触发的外部运行面，不能以本机无调用日志判定闲置。

## UNKNOWN / Stop Conditions

出现任一项即停止进入删除：

1. 路径仍有精确代码、workflow、测试、manifest 或文档引用。
2. 目录由 glob、computed path、changed-file routing 动态读取。
3. `.app`、LaunchAgent、Codex automation、GitHub issue/repository-dispatch 等仓外消费者未核验。
4. 候选属于 active project，owner/root-lock/current task 未明确迁移授权。
5. current release/HANDOFF/deployment 证据链尚未收敛到替代源。
6. TaskPack/review bundle 的引用 manifest、测试和恢复方式未作为同一原子批次设计。
7. `replacement_source` 或 `runtime_dependency` 仍为 `UNKNOWN_PENDING_S02_P2`。
8. 无法证明 `git revert <S03 commit>` 可完整恢复引用链。

## 进程与副作用证据

<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](http[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]

### Workspace Dependencies
- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.

### Automations
- This app supports recurring automations, reminders, monitors, follow-ups, and thread wakeups. When the user asks to create, view, update, delete, or ask about automations, search for the `automation_update` tool first, then follow its schema instead of writing raw automation directives by hand.
- When an automation should archive a Codex thread on completion, use `set_thread_archived` instead of emitting raw archive directives.

### Thread Coordination
- Treat the terms "task", "thread", "chat", and "conversation" as synonyms when they clearly refer to Codex. Tool names use the term "thread" and Codex uses "task" in the UI. When providing user-facing responses, use "task".
- When the user asks to create, fork, inspect, continue, hand off, pin, archive, rename, or otherwise manage Codex threads, search for the relevant thread tool first: `create_thread`, `fork_thread`, `list_threads`, `read_thread`, `send_message_to_thread`, `handoff_thread`, `set_thread_pinned`, `set_thread_archived`, or `set_thread_title`.
- Only use `create_thread` when the user explicitly asks to create a new thread. Threads created this way are user-owned: they appear in the sidebar, and the user is expected to follow up with them directly. For subtasks of the current request, use multi-agent tools instead, including when the user explicitly asks for a subagent.
- After a successful `create_thread` call, emit `::created-thread{threadId="..."}` for a created thread or `::created-thread{clientThreadId="..."}` for queued worktree setup on its own line in your final response.

### Inline Code Comments
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive when you need to attach feedback directly to specific code lines.
- Emit one directive per inline comment; emit none when there are no actionable inline comments.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3).
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2}
</app-context>

Each entry includes a name, description, and source locator. `file` locators are on the host filesystem, `environment resource` locators are owned by an execution environment, `orchestrator resource` locators are opaque non-filesystem resources, and `custom resource` locators use their provider's access mechanism.

## Memory

You have access to a memory folder with guidance from prior runs. It can save
time and help you stay consistent. Use it whenever it is likely to help.

Decision boundary: should you use memory for a new user query?

- Skip memory ONLY when the request is clearly self-contained and does not need
  workspace history, conventions, or prior decisions.
- Hard skip examples: current time/date, simple translation, simple sentence
  rewrite, one-line shell command, trivial formatting.
- Use memory by default when ANY of these are true:
  - the query mentions workspace/repo/module/path/files in MEMORY_SUMMARY below,
  - the user asks for prior context / consistency / previous decisions,
  - the task is ambiguous and could depend on earlier project choices,
  - the ask is a non-trivial and related to MEMORY_SUMMARY below.
- If unsure, do a quick memory pass.

- [REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH] (already provided below; do NOT open again)

  - These files are append-only `jsonl`: `session_meta.payload.id` identifies the session, `turn_context` marks turn boundaries, `event_msg` is the lightweight status stream, and `response_item` contains actual messages, tool calls, and tool outputs.
  - For efficient lookup, prefer matching the filename suffix or `session_meta.payload.id`; avoid broad full-content scans unless needed.

Quick memory pass (when applicable):

1. Skim the MEMORY_SUMMARY below and extract task-relevant keywords.

4. If above are not clear and you need exact commands, error text, or precise evidence, search over `rollout_path` for more evidence.
5. If there are no relevant hits, stop memory lookup and continue normally.

Quick-pass budget:

- Keep memory lookup lightweight: ideally <= 4-6 search steps before main work.
- Avoid broad scans of all rollout summaries.

During execution: if you hit repeated errors, confusing behavior, or suspect
relevant prior context, redo the quick memory pass.

How to decide whether to verify memory:

- Consider both risk of drift and verification effort.
- If a fact is likely to drift and is cheap to verify, verify it before
  answering.
- If a fact is likely to drift but verification is expensive, slow, or
  disruptive, it is acceptable to answer from memory in an interactive turn,
  but you should say that it is memory-derived, note that it may be stale, and
  consider offering to refresh it live.
- If a fact is lower-drift and expensive to verify, it is usually fine to
  answer from memory directly.

When answering from memory without current verification:

- If you rely on memory for a fact that you did not verify in the current turn,
  say so briefly in the final answer.
- If that fact is plausibly drift-prone or comes from an older note, older
  snapshot, or prior run summary, say that it may be stale or outdated.
- If live verification was skipped and a refresh would be useful in the
  interactive context, consider offering to verify or refresh it live.
- Do not present unverified memory-derived facts as confirmed-current.
- Prefer a short refresh offer for interactive questions, especially about prior
  results, commands, timing, or older snapshots.

You are an agent in a team of agents collaborating to complete a task.

You can spawn sub-agents to handle subtasks, and those sub-agents can spawn their own sub-agents. All agents in the team, including the agents that you can assign tasks to, are equally intelligent and capable, and have access to the same set of tools.

You can use `spawn_agent` to create a new agent, `followup_task` to give an existing agent a new task and trigger a turn, and `send_message` to pass a message to a running agent.
Child agents can also spawn their own sub-agents.

When you provide a response in the final channel, that content is immediately delivered back to your parent agent.

You will receive messages in the analysis channel in the form:
```
Message Type: NEW_TASK | MESSAGE | FINAL_ANSWER
Task name: <recipient>
Sender: <author>
Payload:
<payload text>
```
You may also see them addressed as to=/root/..., which indicates your identity is /root/...

Note that collaboration tools cannot be called from inside `functions.exec`. Call `spawn_agent`, `send_message`, `followup_task`, `wait_agent`, `interrupt_agent`, and `list_agents` only as direct tool calls using the recipient shown in their tool definitions, such as `to=functions.collaboration.spawn_agent`, since they are intentionally absent from the `functions.exec` `tools.*` namespace. Available tools in `functions.exec` are explicitly described with a `tools` namespace in the developer message.

All agents share the same directory. In detail:
- All agents have access to the same container and filesystem as you.
- All agents use the same current working directory.
- As a result, edits made by one agent are immediately visible to all other agents.

There are 4 available concurrency slots, meaning that up to 4 agents can be active at once, including you.

<INSTRUCTIONS>
## 1. 核心产出标准
所有产出第一追求最大程度实现：准确、真实、可执行、高质量、高 ROI、低Token浪费、低上下文成本、快速迭代、可维护、可验证、能支持真实决策或实际落地。
不要制造低价值文件、伪进度、无意义策略、无依据结论、宽泛重构或为了显得忙碌而做的动作。
默认中文回复；代码、API、库名、错误信息、英文项目语境可保留英文。
执行中不要反复打断。每个有意义的 run 结束时输出：
- 进度百分比
- 已完成
- 未完成
- 预计剩余迭代次数、时间与置信度
- 推荐下一步

## 2. 交互方式
复杂任务不要让我大量自由文本输入。优先使用：
- 编号选择题
- 多选矩阵
- 默认推荐项
- 当前步骤状态表
- 明确假设、验收标准、停止条件
能用 Codex 原生结构化 UI 交互时优先使用

## 3. 任务路由
涉及 app、网站、平台、clone、MVP、PRD、Tech Spec、Task Pack、Run Contract、系统设计等模糊需求时：
- 优先使用 `$codex-dev-orchestrator`
- 不要仅凭模糊关键词直接写代码。
- 默认流程：证据收集 → 逆向产品/研究摘要 → 结构化确认 → PRD/MVP → Tech Spec/Task Pack → 每轮只执行一个 Run Contract → 最终交付。
任何开发开发任务都需要有明确的开发记录文件、功能清单文件和模型参数文件，过往至今所有的开发任务（已解决、未解决），涵盖的参数、目标、对象、范围、风险与管控，验收标准，功能架构，操作流程，交付和运行方式等，模型、公式、参数的数值原理逻辑等。

## 4. Execution workflow
改代码或文件前，通常先给compact execution contract：
1. 目标
2. 最小相关范围
3. 将检查的文件/目录
4. 可能修改的文件
5. 验证命令
6. 风险与回滚
7. 停止条件
低风险、范围清楚、可逆、本地 workspace 内动作可直接推进无需授权申请。
默认可执行：读文件、仓库内搜索、创建/编辑当前任务范围内文件、运行目标测试/类型检查/lint/build、生成本地草稿/报告/Task Pack/HANDOFF、Git commit、push、merge、PR。
授权不清时，先产出本地计划或草稿。

## 5. 上下文与成本效率
默认低上下文、低成本执行。
复杂或继续任务开始时：
1. 先读最新 `HANDOFF.md`。
2. 用实际文件、测试、配置验证。
3. 仅在必要时读取更大上下文、旧日志、历史对话或大目录。
避免全仓扫描，除非任务需要。
会话变长时：同任务建议 `/compact`；新任务或上下文污染建议新 thread；优先写简短 handoff，不拖长历史。
MCP、browser、Playwright、外部研究、多 agent、重工具只在明显增值时使用；无关工具不要加载。产出质量、ROI、生产效率比节省Token更重要。

## 6. HANDOFF.md
复杂、多轮、跨文件、可能换线程继续的任务，可维护简洁 `HANDOFF.md`。不要每轮默认更新；仅在目标稳定、关键决策、重要文件变更、验证完成、阶段切换、暂停/交接、用户要求、或明显有未来价值时更新/建议更新。
- 当前目标
- 当前状态
- 关键决策
- 已改/需看文件
- 验证命令与结果
- 未解决风险
- 下一步
- 必要时 timestamp

## 7. 证据、时效与真实性
不要伪造确定性、进度、数据、来源、测试结果、文件或验证。证据不足时明确不确定性，降级结论，提出最小验证。
涉及当前事实、API、版本、价格、产品行为、政策、法律/监管、可能变化的信息：优先查官方/一手来源，并在报告/研究中引用。

## 8. 工程标准
优先：
- 最小高置信 diff
- 模块化、可复用、可测试
- 幂等操作
- 明确错误处理
- 回滚/补偿路径
- 日志、指标、告警、可观测性
- 边界处权限与安全检查
避免：
- 未要求的大重构
- 不必要依赖
- 静默失败
- 隐式全局状态
- 超大文件/函数
- 深层嵌套
- 命名不清
- 硬编码 secrets 或环境常量
API/服务默认考虑：一致响应结构、schema validation、参数化查询、authn/authz、必要 rate limit、用户安全错误信息与服务端详细日志。

## 9. 测试与验证
先做最小有用验证。默认顺序：
1. 静态检查 / type check
2. 目标 unit test
3. 目标 integration test
4. 相关 build
5. 关键路径 E2E
6. 有理由时才跑 full suite
Bug fix 和核心逻辑新增优先 regression/test-first。未实际运行不得声称通过。测试失败时先判断环境、隔离、mock、测试本身或实现问题；通常先修实现。保留关键信息，避免长日志；用 quiet、short traceback、tail 等短输出。

## 10. 安全基线
安全敏感变更完成前检查：
- 无硬编码 secrets / tokens / passwords / API keys
- 边界输入已验证
- SQL 使用参数化查询
- HTML/user content 考虑 XSS
- 状态变更 web action 考虑 CSRF
- 受保护动作有 authn/authz
- 用户错误不泄露敏感信息
- 相关场景考虑 rate limit / abuse control
- 依赖变化时审查风险
发现严重安全问题：停止扩张 → 说明问题 → 先修 critical/high → 如可能泄露建议 rotate secrets → 必要时查相似模式。

## 11. Skills、agents、ECC、MCP
若项目存在 skills、subagents、commands、ECC-style tooling：
- 只在相关时使用。
- 可复用工作流优先 skills。
- 重要代码、安全、构建、架构变更可用专门 reviewer/agent。
- 不把长 agent/skill 清单加载进上下文，按需发现。
- 独立工作流才并行 agents，且边界清楚。
- 若工具不可用，采用最接近的手动流程并简短说明 fallback。

MCP 和外部集成会增加上下文与风险：
- 改配置前先检查现状。
- 不全局添加重 MCP，除非任务明确需要。
- 保留用户自定义 config、env、credentials、项目本地设置。
- 项目工具优先项目本地配置。
- 默认只读，写操作需明确授权。
</INSTRUCTIONS>

  <shell>zsh</shell>

  <timezone>Australia/Sydney</timezone>

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring skill invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
---

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, ignore this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action** — including clarifying questions, exploring the codebase, or checking files. If it turns out wrong for the situation, you don't have to use it.

**Before entering plan mode:** if you haven't already brainstormed, invoke the brainstorming skill first.

Then announce "Using [skill] to [purpose]" and follow the skill exactly. If it has a checklist, create a todo per item.

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills apply, process skills come first — they set the approach, then implementation skills (frontend-design, etc.) carry it out. Brainstorming and systematic-debugging are Superpowers' most common process skills, but the rule holds for any of them.

- "Let's build X" → superpowers:brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
- "Fix this bug" → superpowers:systematic-debugging first, then domain skills.

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOP—you're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Platform Adaptation

If your harness appears here, read its reference file for special instructions:

- Codex: `references/codex-tools.md`
- Pi: `references/pi-tools.md`
- Antigravity: `references/antigravity-tools.md`

## User Instructions

User instructions (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, etc, direct requests) take precedence over skills, which in turn override default behavior. Only skip skill workflows or instructions when your human partner has explicitly told you to.
---
name: systematic-debugging
description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
---

# Systematic Debugging

## Overview

Random fixes waste time and create new bugs. Quick patches mask underlying issues.

**Core principle:** ALWAYS find root cause before attempting fixes. Symptom fixes are failure.

**Violating the letter of this process is violating the spirit of debugging.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST
```

If you haven't completed Phase 1, you cannot propose fixes.

## When to Use

Use for ANY technical issue:
- Test failures
- Bugs in production
- Unexpected behavior
- Performance problems
- Build failures
- Integration issues

**Use this ESPECIALLY when:**
- Under time pressure (emergencies make guessing tempting)
- "Just one quick fix" seems obvious
- You've already tried multiple fixes
- Previous fix didn't work
- You don't fully understand the issue

**Don't skip when:**
- Issue seems simple (simple bugs have root causes too)
- You're in a hurry (rushing guarantees rework)
- Manager wants it fixed NOW (systematic is faster than thrashing)

## The Four Phases

You MUST complete each phase before proceeding to the next.

### Phase 1: Root Cause Investigation

**BEFORE attempting ANY fix:**

1. **Read Error Messages Carefully**
   - Don't skip past errors or warnings
   - They often contain the exact solution
   - Read stack traces completely
   - Note line numbers, file paths, error codes

2. **Reproduce Consistently**
   - Can you trigger it reliably?
   - What are the exact steps?
   - Does it happen every time?
   - If not reproducible → gather more data, don't guess

3. **Check Recent Changes**
   - What changed that could cause this?
   - Git diff, recent commits
   - New dependencies, config changes
   - Environmental differences

4. **Gather Evidence in Multi-Component Systems**

   **WHEN system has multiple components (CI → build → signing, API → service → database):**

   **BEFORE proposing fixes, add diagnostic instrumentation:**
   ```
   For EACH component boundary:
     - Log what data enters component
     - Log what data exits component
     - Verify environment/config propagation
     - Check state at each layer

   Run once to gather evidence showing WHERE it breaks
   THEN analyze evidence to identify failing component
   THEN investigate that specific component
   ```

   **Example (multi-layer system):**
   ```bash
   # Layer 1: Workflow
   echo "=== Secrets available in workflow: ==="
   echo "IDENTITY: ${IDENTITY:+SET}${IDENTITY:-UNSET}"

   # Layer 2: Build script
   echo "=== Env vars in build script: ==="
   env | grep IDENTITY || echo "IDENTITY not in environment"

   # Layer 3: Signing script
   echo "=== Keychain state: ==="
   security list-keychains
   security find-identity -v

   # Layer 4: Actual signing
   codesign --sign "$IDENTITY" --verbose=4 "$APP"
   ```

   **This reveals:** Which layer fails (secrets → workflow ✓, workflow → build ✗)

5. **Trace Data Flow**

   **WHEN error is deep in call stack:**

   See `root-cause-tracing.md` in this directory for the complete backward tracing technique.

   **Quick version:**
   - Where does bad value originate?
   - What called this with bad value?
   - Keep tracing up until you find the source
   - Fix at source, not at symptom

### Phase 2: Pattern Analysis

**Find the pattern before fixing:**

1. **Find Working Examples**
   - Locate similar working code in same codebase
   - What works that's similar to what's broken?

2. **Compare Against References**
   - If implementing pattern, read reference implementation COMPLETELY
   - Don't skim - read every line
   - Understand the pattern fully before applying

3. **Identify Differences**
   - What's different between working and broken?
   - List every difference, however small
   - Don't assume "that can't matter"

4. **Understand Dependencies**
   - What other components does this need?
   - What settings, config, environment?
   - What assumptions does it make?

### Phase 3: Hypothesis and Testing

**Scientific method:**

1. **Form Single Hypothesis**
   - State clearly: "I think X is the root cause because Y"
   - Write it down
   - Be specific, not vague

2. **Test Minimally**
   - Make the SMALLEST possible change to test hypothesis
   - One variable at a time
   - Don't fix multiple things at once

3. **Verify Before Continuing**
   - Did it work? Yes → Phase 4
   - Didn't work? Form NEW hypothesis
   - DON'T add more fixes on top

4. **When You Don't Know**
   - Say "I don't understand X"
   - Don't pretend to know
   - Ask for help
   - Research more

### Phase 4: Implementation

**Fix the root cause, not the symptom:**

1. **Create Failing Test Case**
   - Simplest possible reproduction
   - Automated test if possible
   - One-off test script if no framework
   - MUST have before fixing
   - Use the `superpowers:test-driven-development` skill for writing proper failing tests

2. **Implement Single Fix**
   - Address the root cause identified
   - ONE change at a time
   - No "while I'm here" improvements
   - No bundled refactoring

3. **Verify Fix**
   - Test passes now?
   - No other tests broken?
   - Issue actually resolved?

4. **If Fix Doesn't Work**
   - STOP
   - Count: How many fixes have you tried?
   - If < 3: Return to Phase 1, re-analyze with new information
   - **If ≥ 3: STOP and question the architecture (step 5 below)**
   - DON'T attempt Fix #4 without architectural discussion

5. **If 3+ Fixes Failed: Question Architecture**

   **Pattern indicating architectural problem:**
   - Each fix reveals new shared state/coupling/problem in different place
   - Fixes require "massive refactoring" to implement
   - Each fix creates new symptoms elsewhere

   **STOP and question fundamentals:**
   - Is this pattern fundamentally sound?
   - Are we "sticking with it through sheer inertia"?
   - Should we refactor architecture vs. continue fixing symptoms?

   **Discuss with your human partner before attempting more fixes**

   This is NOT a failed hypothesis - this is a wrong architecture.

## Red Flags - STOP and Follow Process

If you catch yourself thinking:
- "Quick fix for now, investigate later"
- "Just try changing X and see if it works"
- "Add multiple changes, run tests"
- "Skip the test, I'll manually verify"
- "It's probably X, let me fix that"
- "I don't fully understand but this might work"
- "Pattern says X but I'll adapt it differently"
- "Here are the main problems: [lists fixes without investigation]"
- Proposing solutions before tracing data flow
- **"One more fix attempt" (when already tried 2+)**
- **Each fix reveals new problem in different place**

**ALL of these mean: STOP. Return to Phase 1.**

**If 3+ fixes failed:** Question the architecture (see Phase 4.5)

## your human partner's Signals You're Doing It Wrong

**Watch for these redirections:**
- "Is that not happening?" - You assumed without verifying
- "Will it show us...?" - You should have added evidence gathering
- "Stop guessing" - You're proposing fixes without understanding
- "Ultra-think this" - Question fundamentals, not just symptoms
- "We're stuck?" (frustrated) - Your approach isn't working

**When you see these:** STOP. Return to Phase 1.

## Common Rationalizations

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Issue is simple, don't need process" | Simple issues have root causes too. Process is fast for simple bugs. |
| "Emergency, no time for process" | Systematic debugging is FASTER than guess-and-check thrashing. |
| "Just try this first, then investigate" | First fix sets the pattern. Do it right from the start. |
| "I'll write test after confirming fix works" | Untested fixes don't stick. Test first proves it. |
| "Multiple fixes at once saves time" | Can't isolate what worked. Causes new bugs. |
| "Reference too long, I'll adapt the pattern" | Partial understanding guarantees bugs. Read it completely. |
| "I see the problem, let me fix it" | Seeing symptoms ≠ understanding root cause. |
| "One more fix attempt" (after 2+ failures) | 3+ failures = architectural problem. Question pattern, don't fix again. |

## Quick Reference

| Phase | Key Activities | Success Criteria |

## 验证结果

抽样 5 项覆盖 validator、重复 review、旧 review 文档、ZIP、tar.gz：

- partial clone 中部分旧历史 blob 不在本机：抽样历史缺失对象计数为 `1/0/1/190/0`；因此当前 source snapshot 可恢复，不代表任意历史版本均可离线恢复。

## 建议的 `history_policy.md`

### 1. 核心原则

- Git history 恢复旧记录；当前 tree 不新增 `archive/legacy/history` 复制目录。
- 不执行 history rewrite、force-push、rebase 清理或对象 GC。
- S02 只提供恢复证明；只有 P2-T1、P2-T2、P3 和用户审批全部通过后，S03 才能删除。
- raw archive、当前 release/rollback evidence、其他项目活跃 owner/runtime 资产不得仅因“Git 可恢复”而删除。

这符合 [S02-P2-T3]([REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH] 与 [清理禁止项]([REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH] 2. 每个可删/可合并路径的强制恢复证据

最终 disposition 必须记录：

- `pre_cleanup_base_commit`
- 原路径及所有已知 previous/new paths
- base tree 的 mode/type/object id，不记录正文
- disposition 与 replacement source
- 静态、动态、workflow、runtime 引用结果
- exact restore command
- batch commit 与 inverse action
- partial-clone object availability
- owner approval 与验证命令

S03 preflight 时重新固定实际 `BASE`。若 `07a6e50d…` 到实际 `BASE` 之间候选路径发生变化，原 P1 restore command 作废，必须重新审计。

### 3. 恢复顺序

首选整批回滚：

```bash
git revert --no-edit <S03_BATCH_COMMIT>
```

多个相互依赖批次必须按逆序逐个 revert，每次重新运行该批 validation。禁止 `reset --hard`、rebase 或 force-push。

选择性恢复仅用于后续提交已使整体 revert 冲突时：

```bash
git restore --source=<PRE_CLEANUP_BASE> -- <original-path>
```

对 sparse-excluded 路径：

```bash
git restore --ignore-skip-worktree-bits \
  --source=<PRE_CLEANUP_BASE> -- <original-path>
```

恢复后必须作为新的、可审查 recovery commit 提交；不得直接覆盖后宣称完成。

### 4. Rename / migrate / merge

Git 不原生保存“rename 关系”；rename 是 diff 推断结果。因此：

- `migrate/merge` 必须记录 old path、new/canonical path 和双向 inverse operation。
- 只恢复 old path 不会自动删除 new path，也不会恢复引用关系。
- 对 migrate/merge，优先 revert 整个原子 batch。
- 当前候选 schema 没有 previous-path/rename-chain 字段，故现有 restore string 只证明能取回旧路径，不能证明逻辑迁移可逆。

### 5. Merge commit

- 从 merge commit 的最终 tree 恢复单个路径是确定的。
- 从 merge 历史追溯“原始来源”并不唯一，不能只取 first-parent。
- S03 cleanup commit 必须是单父、非 merge commit。
- 若 S03 意外形成 merge commit，停止；不得猜测 `git revert -m 1`。必须先明确 mainline parent 和两父 tree 差异。

### 6. Partial clone 与大文件

当前 `blob:none` partial clone 不保证任意旧 blob 离线存在。删除前必须对 exact base object 做存在性检查；缺失时：

- 在获准环境中先取回并验证 exact object；或
- 将候选改为 `keep`；不得把“未来可联网 fetch”当作离线恢复证明。

对 ZIP/tar.gz/数据库/媒体：

- 同一既有 blob 被 revert 恢复时通常不会生成新 blob，但仍可能触发大下载和 checkout 成本。
- 不得重新打包成新的 archive blob“备份一次”。
- 若是 LFS pointer，Git history 只恢复 pointer；必须另证 LFS object 可用。
- 本轮禁止尺寸读取，因此大文件阈值与 LFS 完整性仍为 UNKNOWN，不能据此批准删除。

### 7. Git history 不足的情形

出现任一项即不得删除：

- 文件从未 committed，或只存在于 ignored/local/runtime 路径。
- exact base object 在 partial clone 中缺失且远端不可验证取回。
- LFS pointer 存在但 LFS object 不可用。
- 外部数据库、对象存储、submodule commit、加密密钥或 restore tool 不可用。
- 恢复只能得到静态 snapshot，不能恢复必要运行状态或引用。
- rename/migrate 的 old/new path 与引用逆操作不明确。
- 历史已被改写、浅克隆缺少所需 ancestry，或 source commit 不再可达。
- 属于 raw、当前 release/rollback evidence 或其他项目 owner-protected 路径。
- 实际 restore rehearsal 失败。

此时只能 `keep`，或经用户单独授权后使用仓库外的不可变备份；不得在仓库内建立 archive/legacy/history。

### 8. S03 原子提交与回滚门禁

建议五批各形成一个单父原子 commit：

1. documents
2. governance
3. frontend/scripts
4. validation
5. generated

每批必须：

- 只包含获批路径及该批必要引用修复。
- 基于同一已确认 BASE，记录 parent commit。
- commit 前后运行目标 validation。
- 用 path metadata 确认 diff 与获批清单完全一致。
- 在临时 clean clone 中完成 apply→validate→revert→validate rehearsal。
- 可独立 revert，不依赖未提交状态。
- 最终按当前主任务授权一次性 push；不 force、不重写历史。
- push 后若回滚，使用新的 revert commit 并再次验证 remote HEAD。

const r = await tools.exec_command({
  cmd: "ruby -rjson -rset -e '
cs=JSON.parse(File.read(%q{/tmp/openaidatabase_v121_s02_p1_07a6e50d/cleanup_candidates.json}))[%q{candidates}]
paths=cs.map{|x|x[%q{path}]}.select{|p|p.start_with?(%q{governance/run_manifests/})}.to_set
old=paths.select do |p|
 t=File.binread(p).force_encoding(%q{UTF-8}).scrub
 t.include?(%q{FINAL_ACCEPTANCE_BUNDLE}) || t.include?(%q{HANDOFF/00_下一Agent先读.md}) || t.include?(%q{CF-L[REDACTED_PHONE]}) || t.include?(%q{scripts/cloudflare/validate_compatibility_envelope.py})
end.to_set
base=paths.select do |p|
 t=File.binread(p).force_encoding(%q{UTF-8}).scrub
 p==%q{governance/run_manifests/CF-L[REDACTED_PHONE].json} || t.include?(%q{FINAL_ACCEPTANCE_BUNDLE/}) || t.include?(%q{"HANDOFF/}) || t.include?(%q{CF-L2})
end.to_set
strong=Set[
%q{governance/run_manifests/ADP-S2PLT02-NORMALIZED-REAL-DELIVERY-MANIFEST-20260629.json},
%q{governance/run_manifests/ADP-S2PLT02-NORMALIZED-REAL-DELIVERY-MANIFEST-20260630.json},
%q{governance/run_manifests/ADP-S2PLT02-REAL-SCHEDULER-PROOF-20260701.json},
%q{governance/run_manifests/ADP-S2PLT03-LOCAL-RESILIENCE-DRILL-20260628.json},
%q{governance/run_manifests/ADP-S2PLT03-RESILIENCE-PRECHECK-20260628.json},
%q{governance/run_manifests/ADP-S2PMT07-AUTHORIZED-CONTROLLED-REAL-RUN-ACCEPTANCE-20260701.json}
]
newset=base|strong
puts({paths:paths.size,old:old.size,base:base.size,strong:strong.size,strong_overlap:(base&strong).size,new:newset.size,intersection:(old&newset).size,old_only:(old-newset).size,new_only:(newset-old).size}.to_json)
puts %q{--- OLD_ONLY ---}; (old-newset).sort.each{|p|puts p}
puts %q{--- NEW_ONLY ---}; (newset-old).sort.each{|p|puts p}
puts %q{--- CF-L2 outside FAB ---}; paths.select{|p|File.binread(p).include?(%q{CF-L2}) && !File.binread(p).include?(%q{FINAL_ACCEPTANCE_BUNDLE/})}.sort.each{|p|puts p}
' ",
  workdir: "[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]",
  yield_time_ms: 30000,
  max_output_tokens: 30000
});
text(r.output);

---
name: verification-before-completion
description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
---

# Verification Before Completion

## Overview

Claiming work is complete without verification is dishonesty, not efficiency.

**Core principle:** Evidence before claims, always.

**Violating the letter of this rule is violating the spirit of this rule.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE
```

If you haven't run the verification command in this message, you cannot claim it passes.

## The Gate Function

```
BEFORE claiming any status or expressing satisfaction:

1. IDENTIFY: What command proves this claim?
2. RUN: Execute the FULL command (fresh, complete)
3. READ: Full output, check exit code, count failures
4. VERIFY: Does output confirm the claim?
   - If NO: State actual status with evidence
   - If YES: State claim WITH evidence
5. ONLY THEN: Make the claim

Skip any step = lying, not verifying
```

## Common Failures

| Claim | Requires | Not Sufficient |
|-------|----------|----------------|
| Tests pass | Test command output: 0 failures | Previous run, "should pass" |
| Linter clean | Linter output: 0 errors | Partial check, extrapolation |
| Build succeeds | Build command: exit 0 | Linter passing, logs look good |
| Bug fixed | Test original symptom: passes | Code changed, assumed fixed |
| Regression test works | Red-green cycle verified | Test passes once |
| Agent completed | VCS diff shows changes | Agent reports "success" |
| Requirements met | Line-by-line checklist | Tests passing |

## Red Flags - STOP

- Using "should", "probably", "seems to"
- Expressing satisfaction before verification ("Great!", "Perfect!", "Done!", etc.)
- About to commit/push/PR without verification
- Trusting agent success reports
- Relying on partial verification
- Thinking "just this once"
- Tired and wanting work over
- **ANY wording implying success without having run verification**

## Rationalization Prevention

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Should work now" | RUN the verification |
| "I'm confident" | Confidence ≠ evidence |
| "Just this once" | No exceptions |
| "Linter passed" | Linter ≠ compiler |
| "Agent said success" | Verify independently |
| "I'm tired" | Exhaustion ≠ excuse |
| "Partial check is enough" | Partial proves nothing |
| "Different words so rule doesn't apply" | Spirit over letter |

## Key Patterns

**Tests:**
```
✅ [Run test command] [See: 34/34 pass] "All tests pass"
❌ "Should pass now" / "Looks correct"
```

**Regression tests (TDD Red-Green):**
```
✅ Write → Run (pass) → Revert fix → Run (MUST FAIL) → Restore → Run (pass)
❌ "I've written a regression test" (without red-green verification)
```

**Build:**
```
✅ [Run build] [See: exit 0] "Build passes"
❌ "Linter passed" (linter doesn't check compilation)
```

**Requirements:**
```
✅ Re-read plan → Create checklist → Verify each → Report gaps or completion
❌ "Tests pass, phase complete"
```

**Agent delegation:**
```
✅ Agent reports success → Check VCS diff → Verify changes → Report actual state
❌ Trust agent report
```

## Why This Matters

From 24 failure memories:
- your human partner said "I don't believe you" - trust broken
- Undefined functions shipped - would crash
- Missing requirements shipped - incomplete features
- Time wasted on false completion → redirect → rework
- Violates: "Honesty is a core value. If you lie, you'll be replaced."

## When To Apply

**ALWAYS before:**
- ANY variation of success/completion claims
- ANY expression of satisfaction
- ANY positive statement about work state
- Committing, PR creation, task completion
- Moving to next task
- Delegating to agents

**Rule applies to:**
- Exact phrases
- Paraphrases and synonyms
- Implications of success
- ANY communication suggesting completion/correctness

## The Bottom Line

**No shortcuts for verification.**

Run the command. Read the output. THEN claim the result.

This is non-negotiable.

const r = await tools.exec_command({
  cmd: "set -euo pipefail
export GIT_NO_LAZY_FETCH=1
fixed=$(git rev-parse '07a6e50d^{commit}')
origin=$(git rev-parse 'origin/main^{commit}')

---
name: github
description: Triage and orient GitHub repository, pull request, and issue work through the connected GitHub app. Use when the user asks for general GitHub help, wants PR or issue summaries, or needs repository context before choosing a more specific GitHub workflow.
---

# GitHub

## Overview

Use this skill as the umbrella entrypoint for general GitHub work in this plugin. It should decide whether the task stays in repo and PR triage or should be handed off to a more specific review, CI, or publish workflow.

This plugin is intentionally hybrid:

- Prefer the GitHub app from this plugin for repository, issue, pull request, comment, label, reaction, and PR creation workflows.
- Use local `git` and `gh` only when the connector does not cover the job well, especially for current-branch PR discovery, branch creation, commit and push, `gh auth status`, and GitHub Actions log inspection.
- Keep connector state and local checkout context aligned. If the request is about the current branch, resolve the local repo and branch before acting.

Once the intent is clear, route to the specialist skill immediately and do not keep broad GitHub triage in scope longer than needed.

## Connector-First Responsibilities

Handle these directly in this skill when the request does not need a narrower specialist workflow:

- repository orientation once the repo, PR, issue, or local checkout is identified
- recent PR or issue triage
- PR metadata summaries
- PR patch inspection
- PR comments, labels, and reactions
- issue lookup and summarization
- PR creation after a branch is already pushed

Prefer the GitHub app from this plugin for those flows because it provides structured PR, issue, and review-adjacent data without depending on a local checkout. If the repository is not already identifiable from the user request or local git context, ask for the repo instead of pretending there is a repo-search flow that may not exist.

## Routing Rules

1. Resolve the operating context first:
   - If the user provides a repository, PR number, issue number, or URL, use that.
   - If the request is about "this branch" or "the current PR", resolve local git context and use `gh` only as needed to discover the branch PR.
   - If the repository is still ambiguous after local inspection, ask for the repo identifier.
2. Classify the request before taking action:
   - `repo or PR triage`: summarize PRs, issues, patches, comments, labels, reactions, or repository state
   - `review follow-up`: unresolved review threads, requested changes, or inline review feedback
   - `CI debugging`: failing checks, Actions logs, or CI root-cause analysis
   - `publish changes`: create or switch branches, stage changes, commit, push, and open a draft PR
3. Route to the specialist skill as soon as the category is clear:
   - Review comments and requested changes: `../gh-address-comments/SKILL.md`
   - Failing GitHub Actions checks: `../gh-fix-ci/SKILL.md`
   - Commit, push, and open PR: `../yeet/SKILL.md`
4. Keep the hybrid model consistent after routing:
   - connector first for PR and issue data
   - local `git` and `gh` only for the specific gaps the connector does not cover

## Default Workflow

1. Resolve repository and item scope.
2. Gather structured PR or issue context through the GitHub app from this plugin.
3. Decide whether the task stays in connector-backed triage or needs a specialist skill.
4. Route immediately when the work becomes review follow-up, CI debugging, or publish workflow.
5. End with a clear summary of what was inspected, what changed, and what remains.

## Output Expectations

- For triage requests, return a concise summary of the repository, PR, or issue state and the next likely action.
- For mixed requests, tell the user which specialist path you are taking and why.
- For connector-backed write actions, restate the exact PR, issue, label, or reaction target before applying the change.
- Never imply that GitHub Actions logs are available through the connector alone. That remains a `gh` workflow.

## Examples

- "Use GitHub to summarize the open PRs in this repo and tell me what needs attention."
- "Help with this PR."
- "Review the latest comments on PR 482 and tell me what is actionable."
- "Debug the failing checks on this branch."
- "Commit these changes, push them, and open a draft PR."

const r = await tools.exec_command({
  cmd: "set -euo pipefail
base='07a6e50d593c7b9c74b8f3870b614be86a87160d'
head='5e48e739774575f4d198d6268271e557de434897'
out='/tmp/openaidatabase_v121_s02_remote_audit_5e48e739'
while IFS=$'\t' read -r status path; do
  mkdir -p "$out/head/$(dirname "$path")"
  encoded=$(jq -rn --arg p "$path" '$p|@uri|gsub("%2F";"/")')
  curl --fail --silent --show-error --location --max-time 30 --user-agent 'Codex-S02-read-only-audit' \
    "http[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]" \
    --output "$out/head/$path"
  if [[ "$status" != 'added' ]]; then
    mkdir -p "$out/base/$(dirname "$path")"
    curl --fail --silent --show-error --location --max-time 30 --user-agent 'Codex-S02-read-only-audit' \
      "http[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]" \
      --output "$out/base/$path"
  fi
done < <(jq -r '.files[] | [.status,.filename] | @tsv' "$out/compare.json")
head_bad=0
base_bad=0
while IFS=$'\t' read -r status path expected; do
  actual=$(git hash-object --stdin < "$out/head/$path")
  if [[ "$actual" != "$expected" ]]; then
    printf 'HEAD_SHA_MISMATCH\t%s\t%s\t%s\n' "$path" "$expected" "$actual"
    head_bad=$((head_bad+1))
  fi
  if [[ "$status" != 'added' ]]; then
    base_expected=$(jq -r --arg p "$path" '.files[] | select(.path==$p) | .blob_sha' /tmp/openaidatabase_v121_s02_p1_07a6e50d/repo_inventory.json)
    base_actual=$(git hash-object --stdin < "$out/base/$path")
    if [[ -z "$base_expected" || "$base_expected" != "$base_actual" ]]; then
      printf 'BASE_SHA_MISMATCH\t%s\t%s\t%s\n' "$path" "$base_expected" "$base_actual"
      base_bad=$((base_bad+1))
    fi
  fi
done < <(jq -r '.files[] | [.status,.filename,.sha] | @tsv' "$out/compare.json")
printf 'downloaded_head=34 downloaded_base=%s head_sha_mismatch=%s base_sha_mismatch=%s\n' "$(find "$out/base/PFI" -type f | wc -l | tr -d ' ')" "$head_bad" "$base_bad"
test "$head_bad" -eq 0
test "$base_bad" -eq 0",
  workdir: "[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]",
  yield_time_ms: 30000,
  max_output_tokens: 20000
});
text(r.output);

const r = await tools.exec_command({
  cmd: "python3 - <<'PY'
import collections, importlib.util, json
from pathlib import Path
from pathlib import PurePosixPath

mod_path = Path('/tmp/openaidatabase_v121_s02_p1_07a6e50d/generate_inventory.py')
spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('gen', mod_path)
gen = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(gen)
root = Path('/tmp/openaidatabase_v121_s02_remote_audit_5e48e739')
compare = json.loads((root/'compare.json').read_text())
head_tree = json.loads((root/'head_tree.json').read_text())
inv = json.loads(Path('/tmp/openaidatabase_v121_s02_p1_07a6e50d/repo_inventory.json').read_text())
cleanup = json.loads(Path('/tmp/openaidatabase_v121_s02_p1_07a6e50d/cleanup_candidates.json').read_text())
active = set(inv['summary']['active_projects'])
old_candidates = {x['path']: x for x in cleanup['candidates']}
old_files = {x['path']: x for x in inv['files']}
head_blobs = {x['path']: x for x in head_tree['tree'] if x.get('type') == 'blob'}
by_sha = collections.defaultdict(list)
for p, x in head_blobs.items():
    if not gen.is_raw_append_only_or_session_path(p) and int(x.get('size',0)) > 0:
        by_sha[x['sha']].append(p)
head_dup = {p: sha for sha, ps in by_sha.items() if len(ps)>1 for p in ps}
rows=[]
for f in compare['files']:
    p=f['filename']
    role=gen.classify_role(p)
    tags=set(gen.candidate_tags(p, role))
    duplicate_eligible=role in {'human_document','generated_evidence','other','data'}
    if p in head_dup and duplicate_eligibl[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]        tags.add('exact_blob_duplicate')
    protection=gen.default_protection(p, active)
    is_candidate=bool(tags) and not protection
    if is_candidat[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]        category='keep' if gen.top_level(p) in active and gen.top_level(p)!='OpenAIDatabase' else gen.category_for(tags)
        batch=gen.batch_for(p, role, tags)
    els[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]        category=batch=None
    old=old_files.get(p)
    rows.append({
        'path':p,'status':f['status'],'role':role,
        'old_candidate':p in old_candidates,
        'old_category':old_candidates.get(p,{}).get('category'),
        'old_tags':old.get('candidate_tags') if old else None,
        'old_protection':old.get('protected_reason') if old else None,
        'new_candidate':is_candidate,'new_category':category,'new_tags':sorted(tags),
        'new_protection':protection,'new_batch':batch,
        'head_duplicate_sha':head_dup.get(p),
        'head_duplicate_peers':sorted(x for x in by_sha.get(head_blobs[p]['sha'],[]) if x!=p),
    })
print(json.dumps({
  'changed':len(rows),
  'added':sum(r['status']=='added' for r in rows),
  'candidate_added':[r for r in rows if r['new_candidate'] and not r['old_candidate']],
  'candidate_removed':[r for r in rows if r['old_candidate'] and not r['new_candidate']],
  'candidate_stays':[r for r in rows if r['old_candidate'] and r['new_candidate']],
  'all_rows':rows,
},ensure_ascii=False,indent=2))
PY",
  workdir: "[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]",
  yield_time_ms: 30000,
  max_output_tokens: 40000
});
text(r.output);

import csv,collections,json
p='/tmp/openaidatabase_v121_s02_p2_07a6e50d/disposition_matrix.csv'
with open(p,newline='',encoding='utf-8') as h: rows=list(csv.DictReader(h))
shared=[r for r in rows if r['project_scope']=='shared_repo']
oai=[r for r in rows if r['project_scope']=='openaidatabase']
run=[r for r in shared if r['path'].startswith('governance/run_manifests/')]
print(json.dumps({
 'all_rows':len(rows),
 'shared_rows':len(shared),'shared_dispositions':dict(collections.Counter(r['p2_disposition'] for r in shared)),
 'shared_run_manifests':len(run),'shared_run_manifest_dispositions':dict(collections.Counter(r['p2_disposition'] for r in run)),
 'shared_current_bound':sum(r['current_surface_binding_status']=='CURRENT_BOUND' for r in run),
 'oai_rows':len(oai),'oai_dispositions':dict(collections.Counter(r['p2_disposition'] for r in oai)),
 'oai_p3':dict(collections.Counter(r['p3_eligibility'] for r in oai)),
},ensure_ascii=False,indent=2))
PY",
  workdir: "[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]",
  yield_time_ms: 10000,
  max_output_tokens: 10000
});
text(r.output);

const r = await tools.exec_command({
  cmd: "python3 - <<'PY'
import collections,json
from pathlib import PurePosixPath,Path
r=Path('/tmp/openaidatabase_v121_s02_remote_audit_5e48e739')
inv=json.loads(Path('/tmp/openaidatabase_v121_s02_p1_07a6e50d/repo_inventory.json').read_text())
cmp=json.loads((r/'compare.json').read_text())
tree=json.loads((r/'head_tree.json').read_text())
old=collections.Counter(PurePosixPath(x['path']).name for x in inv['files'])
head=collections.Counter(PurePosixPath(x['path']).name for x in tree['tree'] if x.get('type')=='blob')
added=[x['filename'] for x in cmp['files'] if x['status']=='added']
print(json.dumps([{'path':p,'basename':PurePosixPath(p).name,'old_count':old[PurePosixPath(p).name],'head_count':head[PurePosixPath(p).name]} for p in added],ensure_ascii=False,indent=2))
PY",
  workdir: "[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]",
  yield_time_ms: 10000,
  max_output_tokens: 10000
});
text(r.output);

   [REDACTED_PHONE]	- 整个 CodexProject tracked tree；默认排除 raw 正文内容，仅检查路径、大小、hash 和引用。
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	**允许修改**
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	- 仅仓库外临时报告；不得写入仓库。
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	### P1｜仓库级资产与成本盘点
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	| Task | 动作 | 产物 | Task Acceptance |
   147	|---|---|---|---|
   148	| S02-P1-T1 | 按项目、文件类型、大小、修改时间和引用关系盘点 tracked 文件。 | repo_inventory.json | 覆盖整个仓库，且不给 raw 文本内容建立索引。 |
   149	| S02-P1-T2 | 识别历史 TaskPack、handoff、acceptance bundle、stage 记录、重复报告、构建缓存、生成物和过期快照。 | cleanup_candidates.json | 每项必须有路径、类别、候选动作和证据。 |
   150	| S02-P1-T3 | 识别超大人类文档、巨型入口文件、重复 validator、重复 package scripts 和单用途脚本。 | maintenance_hotspots.md | 量化其对搜索、Codex 上下文、构建和维护的负面影响。 |
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	### P2｜引用、运行与历史价值核验
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	| Task | 动作 | 产物 | Task Acceptance |
   155	|---|---|---|---|
   156	| S02-P2-T1 | 对每个删除候选检查代码引用、文档链接、workflow、部署、测试和数据依赖。 | reference_evidence.csv | 无引用证明不得删除；动态引用需人工证据。 |
   157	| S02-P2-T2 | 将候选分为保留、合并、删除、重新生成、迁移为当前真相五类。 | disposition_matrix.csv | 每项说明理由、替代真相源和恢复方式。 |
   158	| S02-P2-T3 | 确认 Git 历史可承担旧记录归档，不再新建 archive/legacy/history 复制目录。 | history_policy.md | 不重写 Git 历史，不 force-push。 |
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	### P3｜清理批次、风险和验证设计
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	| Task | 动作 | 产物 | Task Acceptance |
   163	|---|---|---|---|
   164	| S02-P3-T1 | 把清理拆成文档、治理、前端/脚本、验证、生成物五个原子批次。 | cleanup_batches.json | 每批可单独提交、验证和 revert。 |
   165	| S02-P3-T2 | 定义保留清单与不可触碰项，特别是其他项目的活跃源代码、数据和发布证据。 | protected_paths.json | 不因“全仓清理”而盲删其他项目活跃内容。 |
   166	| S02-P3-T3 | 输出 S03 将删除/合并/改写的精确文件表和预计净减少量。 | S03 清理计划 | 用户可在不阅读代码的情况下看懂影响。 |
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	### Stage Acceptance Criteria
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	- 整个仓库完成只读盘点，每个候选都有引用证据、替代源和恢复方案。
   171	- 不生成新的历史归档目录，不把清理本身变成新治理负担。
   172	- 明确哪些清理是 OpenAIDatabase 专属、哪些是仓库级共享清理。
   173	- 估算文件数、文档行数、public scripts、validator 和默认 Codex 读取量的净减少。
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	### Stop Conditions
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	- 候选文件存在无法解释的运行时动态引用。
   178	- 需要改写 Git 历史、force push 或删除 remote branch 才能完成。
   179	- 其他项目的 owner 入口或活跃发布状态无法判断。
   180	- 清理计划反而新增等量或更多治理文件。
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	### Validation
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	- git ls-files 与文件大小统计
   185	- rg/grep 静态引用 + workflow/package/config 引用核对
   186	- 现有 build/test/deploy 命令只读基线运行
   187	- 清理候选数量、引用证据和替代源完整性检查
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	### Pass Gate
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	用户认可 S03 的精确清理批次；每个删除候选均有可恢复证据。
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	### Rollback
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	无仓库变更；废弃临时报告即可。
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	## S03｜安全清理当前工作树并合并唯一真相源
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	**所属主线：** L4 全仓清理、治理减负、运行维护与质量门禁  
   200	**Pursuing Goal：** 按获批清单删除当前树中的历史负担、压缩人类记录并合并重复真相源，同时保留 Git 历史、运行能力和可恢复性。  
   201	**执行模式：** 受控写入；通过后原子提交并直接推送 main。
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	### 文件范围
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	**允许读取**
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	- S02 获批清单涉及的整个仓库路径。
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	**允许修改**
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	- 仅 S02 获批的删除、合并和引用修复路径。
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	### P1｜文档与历史记录收敛
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	| Task | 动作 | 产物 | Task Acceptance |
   216	|---|---|---|---|
   217	| S03-P1-T1 | 删除已被 Git 历史覆盖的旧 TaskPack、handoff、stage 逐轮记录和重复验收包。 | 精简后的当前树 | 不得新建 archive 目录复制旧内容。 |
   218	| S03-P1-T2 | 将 开发记录.md 压缩为当前版本摘要，将 CHANGELOG 收敛为版本级变更，将 模型参数文件.md 只保留当前有效参数。 | 三个简洁 owner 文件 | 历史细节通过 Git 历史可恢复；默认阅读不再加载数千行记录。 |
   219	| S03-P1-T3 | 合并或删除人类可读目录中的逐 Stage 文档和重复说明。 | 人类文档候选集 | 为 S05 最终双平面保留不超过 7 个核心入口。 |
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	### P2｜机器治理、生成物与死引用清理
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	| Task | 动作 | 产物 | Task Acceptance |
   224	|---|---|---|---|
   225	| S03-P2-T1 | 删除过期 stage evidence、重复 manifest、旧生成物和当前构建不需要的缓存。 | 精简治理树 | 保留当前 release、最近成功运行和必要回归基线。 |
   226	| S03-P2-T2 | 修复 README、AGENTS、workflow、package、脚本和测试中的失效路径。 | 无断链引用 | rg 检查旧路径为零，动态路径有测试覆盖。 |
   227	| S03-P2-T3 | 生成一份紧凑的 machine cleanup manifest，记录删除路径、理由、替代源和提交。 | cleanup_manifest.json | 仅一份机器文件，不复制旧内容。 |
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	### P3｜清理验证与 main 直推
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	| Task | 动作 | 产物 | Task Acceptance |
   232	|---|---|---|---|
   233	| S03-P3-T1 | 运行完整基线 build/test/deploy dry-run 和引用检查。 | 验证报告 | 任何功能回归都必须在提交前修复或回滚该批。 |
   234	| S03-P3-T2 | 再次 fetch origin/main，确认远端未变化并检查 staged diff。 | push preflight | 不得自动 rebase、merge、force。 |
   235	| S03-P3-T3 | 以原子 commit 直接 push origin main，并验证本地 HEAD 等于 origin/main。 | main 清理提交 | 不创建 branch，不创建 PR。 |
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	### Stage Acceptance Criteria
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	- 删除内容严格限定在 S02 获批清单；没有新建历史归档复制物。
   240	- 开发记录、CHANGELOG、模型参数与人类文档显著压缩，当前真相源唯一。
   241	- 所有现有核心测试、构建和部署 dry-run 通过。
   242	- 提交已直接推送 main，本地 HEAD 与 origin/main 一致。
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	### Stop Conditions
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	- 实际删除范围超过获批清单。
   247	- 清理后出现断链、构建失败、运行时数据缺失或其他项目回归。
   248	- origin/main 在实施期间变化。
   249	- 需要 force push、history rewrite、branch 或 PR。
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	### Validation
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	- git diff --name-status 与 cleanup_manifest 一致
   254	- rg 旧路径/旧版本引用为零或有明确迁移映射
   255	- Python tests + Memory Atlas build + 现有 E2E/部署 dry-run
   256	- git diff --cached --check
   257	- git push origin main 后比较 HEAD 与 origin/main
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	### Pass Gate
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	清理提交已在 main，完整回归通过，用户可从简洁入口理解当前系统。
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	### Rollback
   [REDACTED_PHONE]	使用 git revert 清理提交；禁止 reset --hard 到远端、禁止改写历史。

CLEANUP
     1	# 全仓清理与真相源收敛规范
     2	
     3	## 清理目标
     4	
     5	清理的是当前工作树和默认开发上下文，不是改写历史。Git 历史负责恢复旧记录；当前 main 只保留当前产品、当前治理、必要数据和高价值回归证据。
     6	
     7	## 双阶段原则
     [REDACTED_PHONE]. **S02 只读证明**：整个仓库盘点、引用、运行依赖、替代源、恢复方式。
    10	2. **S03 安全执行**：只删除获批清单，分原子批次验证并 revert-safe 推送 main。
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	## 优先候选
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	- 旧 TaskPack、handoff、final acceptance bundle、阶段恢复包。
    15	- 人类可读目录中的逐 Stage 文档、重复使用说明和机器化状态记录。
    16	- 机器治理中的历史 stage evidence、重复 manifest、旧 run logs 和重复真相源。
    17	- 数千行开发记录、模型参数、CHANGELOG 中的 task-level 历史。
    18	- package.json 中版本/阶段 validator 别名和只验证文件存在的低价值脚本。
    19	- 未挂载组件、死代码、过期 prototype、双实现和构建缓存。
    20	- 根目录/项目目录中已有 Git 历史可恢复、当前无引用的交付 ZIP/目录。
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	## 保留与保护
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	- 当前运行源代码、部署配置、当前数据契约和必要派生数据。
    25	- 当前 release 的验收与恢复证据。
    26	- 其他项目的 owner 入口与活跃源代码；无充分证据不删。
    27	- 用户授权公开的 raw archives；清理不得覆盖、删除或重写 raw。
    28	- 根 AGENTS 与项目 AGENTS 的最小有效指令。
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	## 数量目标（守门而非机械拆分）
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	| 对象 | 目标 |
    33	|---|---|
    34	| 人类可读核心文件 | ≤ 7 |
    35	| App.tsx | 目标 ≤ 800 行，硬上限 1200 行 |
    36	| atlasctl 入口 | 目标 ≤ 400 行，硬上限 600 行 |
    37	| public validation profiles | fast / sync / ui / release 四类 |
    38	| 当前开发记录 | 版本级摘要，建议 ≤ 300 行 |
    39	| 当前模型参数说明 | 只含有效参数与公式，不含 stage 日志 |
    40	| 旧命令兼容 | 最多保留一版，随后删除 |
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	数量不应诱导无意义文件拆分。最终判断是职责、可读性、依赖和测试是否清楚。
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	## 删除证明字段
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	每个候选至少包含：路径、类别、当前引用、运行依赖、替代源、删除原因、恢复方式、所属清理批次、验证命令、审批状态。
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	## 禁止
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	- 新建 archive/legacy/history 目录保存已删除内容。
    51	- git filter-repo、rebase 历史、force push。
    52	- 先删后找引用。
    53	- 清理同时大范围改变产品行为。
    54	- 为记录清理再生成大量人类文档或逐 Task 日志。

GATES
     1	# 开发门禁、停止、回滚与发布协议
     2	
     3	## 默认执行粒度
     4	
     5	一次一个 Stage。用户可以授权连续 Stage 范围，但每个 Stage 必须独立：
     6	
     7	```text
     8	read scope → plan → implement → validate → pass gate → commit → push main → remote verify
     9	```
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	不得因为连续授权而合并 Gate 或跳过 Stop Condition。
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	## 防漂移检查（由 Codex 自己执行，不增加用户负担）
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	每次修改前核对：当前 Stage Goal、允许文件、禁止文件、Acceptance、Stop、Validation。若当前计划无法逐项引用这些内容，停止并重读当前 Stage；无需创建新的 Agent、报告或人类文档。
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	## 轻量 Stage Evidence
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	只保留一个 current evidence 对象：Stage、base commit、修改文件、命令、测试、结果、commit、remote HEAD。发布后覆盖/汇总，不生成逐 Task 历史文件。
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	## Stop 总条件
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	- 不是 main、工作树有未知变更、HEAD 与 origin/main 分叉。
    24	- 当前修改超出 Stage file scope。
    25	- raw source 或既有 raw archive 将被修改/删除/覆盖。
    26	- credentials 将进入 staged diff。
    27	- 任何 required validation 失败。
    28	- remote main 在实施期间变化。
    29	- 需要 branch、PR、force、history rewrite 或未批准外部服务。
    30	- 用户核心路径未验收但机器合同显示绿色。
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	## Validation 层级
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	| Profile | 内容 |
    35	|---|---|
    36	| fast | lint/type/unit/contract/changed-files |
    37	| sync | raw integrity、connector、idempotency、state、manifest、restore、push preflight |
    38	| ui | build、关键组件、中文、语义乱码、E2E、截图、可视状态、性能基础 |
    39	| release | clean clone、全测试、全源 restore、owner-daily、direct-main、live deployment |
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	## 回滚
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	- 代码/配置/文档：使用 `git revert <stage_commit>`。
    44	- 模型/派生：回退 version pointer 后重建。
    45	- UI：短期 feature flag 回退，修复后删除旧实现。
    46	- Scheduler：卸载/disable，保留手动同步。
    47	- Proposal apply：使用 proposal rollback 信息恢复。
    48	- Raw：永不回滚覆盖；用 ledger 标记失败/孤立批次。
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	## 发布条件
    [REDACTED_PHONE]	只有 S16 全部通过才更新 VERSION=1.2.1。不得用“文件已生成”“测试大部分通过”“外部导出还在等待”宣称发布。
    20	S02,stop,2,需要改写 Git 历史、force push 或删除 remote branch 才能完成。
    21	S02,stop,3,其他项目的 owner 入口或活跃发布状态无法判断。
    22	S02,stop,4,清理计划反而新增等量或更多治理文件。
    23	S02,validation,1,git ls-files 与文件大小统计
    24	S02,validation,2,rg/grep 静态引用 + workflow/package/config 引用核对
    25	S02,validation,3,现有 build/test/deploy 命令只读基线运行
    26	S02,validation,4,清理候选数量、引用证据和替代源完整性检查
    27	S03,acceptance,1,删除内容严格限定在 S02 获批清单；没有新建历史归档复制物。
    28	S03,acceptance,2,开发记录、CHANGELOG、模型参数与人类文档显著压缩，当前真相源唯一。
    29	S03,acceptance,3,所有现有核心测试、构建和部署 dry-run 通过。
    30	S03,acceptance,4,提交已直接推送 main，本地 HEAD 与 origin/main 一致。
    31	S03,stop,1,实际删除范围超过获批清单。
    32	S03,stop,2,清理后出现断链、构建失败、运行时数据缺失或其他项目回归。
    33	S03,stop,3,origin/main 在实施期间变化。
    34	S03,stop,4,需要 force push、history rewrite、branch 或 PR。
    35	S03,validation,1,git diff --name-status 与 cleanup_manifest 一致
    36	S03,validation,2,rg 旧路径/旧版本引用为零或有明确迁移映射
    37	S03,validation,3,Python tests + Memory Atlas build + 现有 E2E/部署 dry-run
    38	S03,validation,4,git diff --cached --check
    39	S03,validation,5,git push origin main 后比较 HEAD 与 origin/main
    40	S04,acceptance,1,App.tsx 成为薄壳；目标不超过 800 行，硬上限 1200 行，超限必须有书面理由。
    41	S04,acceptance,2,atlasctl 入口目标不超过 400 行，硬上限 600 行；核心逻辑位于可测试模块。

payload.message: TaskPack 语义已经明确：S03 只能修改 S02 获批的精确删除/合并/引用修复路径，而 `NEEDS_ATOMIC_BATCH_REFERENCE_REWRITE` 仍表示“还缺引用闭包证明”，不能仅凭状态名直接并入本轮 S03。接下来我会验证 7 项是否全部具备完整 corpus、无强/弱引用、无动态信号，并统计其真实净减少量；同时把其余 744 项按 gate × disposition × batch 对账。
